Occupational accident

The University of Helsinki has obtained a more comprehensive insurance for its personnel for the exceptional circumstances from 30th of March 2020.

More information: helsinki.fi

Employees’ occupational accident insurance

The university’s employees are insured for occupational accidents and occupational diseases with LähiTapiola.

Employees should have access to the notice at the workplace.

The insurance number is 351-0926284-K.

The insurance is based on the Workers’ Compensation Act (459/2015).

The insurance company compensates costs resulting from occupational accidents and occupational diseases.

The insurance is valid around the world.

Accident insurance for university students and pupils.

What constitutes an occupational accident?

An occupational accident refers to a sudden, unexpected bodily injury caused by an external factor against the will of the injured party. An accident refers to injury that occurs:

in connection with working or performing a work-related task for the employer

in normal, work-related activities at the workplace premises

in the vicinity of the workplace during lunch hour or a break

while commuting between home and workplace (however not applicable to employees working at home)

in work-related training or recreational activities if the event was organised by the employer and the employer approved the participation.

an accident that has occurred during voluntary sports activities (including UniSport) does not constitute an occupational accident, even if the employer has paid for the activity.

After experiencing an occupational accident

Immediately contact the HR coordinator of your local services.
​​​​- The HR coordinator acts as the contact person for the accident and fills in the electronic insurance certificate in the LähiTapiolan web service.
- The insurance certificate form is also available in English.

The HR coordinator will send the insurance certificate to your mobile phone or provide a written copy when you seek treatment.
- In urgent cases, the insurance certificate can also be sent to the clinic afterwards.

You can seek treatment at any clinic. Medical centre Mehiläinen is the current main partner of the insurance company, and those receiving further treatment are directed to Mehiläinen if available in the patient’s area. LähiTapiola also continues its cooperation with Dextra.

The insurance company will contact you directly to request any additional reports on the accident.

Inform your supervisor without delay of any absence or sick leave caused by the occupational accident.
- Fill in an absence notification through the SAP HR portal and mark occupational accident as the code for the reason for the absence.

Send a medical certificate to the employment services, P.O. Box 53, FI-00014 University of Helsinki.

The university will pay your full salary for 90 calendar days of absence caused by an occupational accident or occupational disease.
- Should the absence due to an accident exceed 90 days, you have the right to receive per diem allowance from the insurance company for one year starting from the date of the accident.

When your claim has been processed, the insurance company will send you the decision.

The medical institution and physician invoice the insurance company directly, but you will need to pay for the pharmaceutical costs.
- Apply for compensation for your costs by sending the scanned receipts and documentation to lttkorvaus@lahitapiola.fi (unencrypted address) or to the encrypted address https://secure.lahitapiola.fi. Add your account number and the accident number stated in the decision. Learn more about reimbursable expenses.

Reporting a serious occupational accident

The supervisor or contact person for accidents must immediately report any serious occupational accidents (excluding accidents taking place on commutes) to the university’s occupational safety and health manager (Pirjo Kekäläinen). The employee have to report the fatal accidents, a serious accident refers to an incident causing a permanent or serious injury to the occupational safety and health division of the Regional State Administrative Agency. Do not move or change anything at the scene of the accident until it has been inspected. In addition to The employer’s responsibility to report occupational accidents is based on the Employment Accidents Insurance Act and the Act on Occupational Safety and Health Enforcement and Cooperation on Occupational Safety and Health at Workplaces (46§).

Puncture accident

First aid:

Rinse the puncture, wound or spatter area with plenty of water (at least 5 min) and then with A12 t solution (denaturated 80% ethanol solution or at least 70% alcohol).

Rinse the eyes and mouth with only water or eyewash.

Let the wound bleed to remove dirt from the wound. Do not squeeze the wounded area.

Contact your occupational healthcare services (metropolitan area tel. +358 (0)10 414 0666) or, outside office hours, the nearest available healthcare unit with emergency services.

Report the accident.

PLEASE NOTE! Meilahti Campus: if the accident includes identifiable human blood exposure, go to the emergency clinic of Haartman hospital.
